title: Sisymbrium procerum
taxonomy: Sisymbrium procerum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It belongs to the genus Sisymbrium, which is a group of annual or biennial plants commonly known as mustards or nasturtiums. Sisymbrium procerum is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found growing in dry, sandy areas. The plant is characterized by its white or pink flowers and slender stems, and is often used as a food source for various insects. Sisymbrium procerum is also sometimes cultivated for its edible leaves and seeds.
